output.final_deliverable:
Missing required evidence to complete the exploration.

The merged results only include an initial web search, and all subsequent “visit” steps failed because no specific starting *news article URL* was selected (placeholders like <TO_FILL_AFTER_SEARCH> were never replaced). As a result, there is no visited news article to extract outbound links from, and no visited primary-source pages (paper, policy document, organization statement) to summarize or relate back to that article.

What evidence we *do* have (from the single search-results list):
- UN “Climate Reports” hub page exists and appears to aggregate reports (including an ILO report titled “Heat at work: Implications for safety and health”) but this is not a news article and was not visited, so details/links cannot be extracted reliably. https://www.un.org/en/climatechange/reports
- A Climate Action Tracker PDF (“CAT_2025-11-13_GlobalUpdate_COP30.pdf”) appears to be a report, not a news article; it was not visited. https://climateactiontracker.org/documents/1348/CAT_2025-11-13_GlobalUpdate_COP30.pdf
- US DOE “Climate” topic page appears to mention a DOE report titled “A Critical Review of Impacts of Greenhouse Gas Emissions on the U.S. Climate”; not a news article and not visited. https://www.energy.gov/topics/climate
- A Nature “climate-change policy” subject page is a topical landing page, not necessarily a single news article; not visited. https://www.nature.com/subjects/climate-change-policy
- Two New York Times items appear to be news articles, but they were not visited, and NYT paywalls often require careful handling; without visits, I can’t extract outbound links or confirm referenced documents:
  - https://www.nytimes.com/2025/04/17/well/trump-nih-cuts-climate-change-health.html
  - https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/10/climate/judge-manual-climate-change-chapter.html

What’s missing (needed to satisfy your mandate):
1) A specific *news article about climate change* that has been visited and whose key claims + outbound links are extracted.
2) A *scientific research paper/study* reached by following a link from that news article, visited and summarized.
3) A *government policy document* reached by following a link from that news article, visited and summarized.
4) An *organization’s position statement* reached by following a link from that news article, visited and summarized.
5) A documented “exploration path” (article → links → three target documents), which requires the above visits.

Because the merged results contain no completed visits, I cannot truthfully provide the requested URLs-with-summaries-and-relationships based on visited evidence.

If you want me to proceed within the benchmark rule, the next step is to run a new search specifically for a climate-change news article that includes outbound links to (a) a journal paper, (b) a .gov policy document, and (c) an NGO/industry group position statement, then visit that article and follow its links.
output.action_summary: The task can’t be completed from the merged results because no starting climate-change news article was selected/visited (the workflow contains URL placeholders), and none of the linked primary sources were visited. Only a list of candidate URLs from an initial search is available, which is insufficient to extract outbound links, summarize documents, or document an exploration path.

merged_results.title: Find a suitable climate-change news article that contains outbound links to primary sources (papers, policy docs, org statements)

result.query: climate change news article links to report study policy document organization statement
result.intent: Locate a climate-change news article that includes outbound links we can follow to a scientific study, a government policy document, and an organization position statement.

results.title: Climate Reports | United Nations
results.description: It provides analysis and guidance to policymakers and practitioners to: (i) facilitate better understanding of the urban focus in NDCs, (ii) highlight climate challenges and opportunities in cities, (iii) support countries to place cities at the center of their climate ambition, and (iv) provide unique climate data to inform policymaking. Report ... “Heat at work: Implications for safety and health,” a report from the International Labour Organization (ILO), warns that more workers are being exposed to heat stress worldwide.

results.title: Policies & action 2030 & 2035 targets only Pledges & targets Optimistic
results.description: On 3 November, China officially submitted a 2035 climate target that is unlikely to drive down · emissions, as the country is already on track to meet it with existing policies.

results.title: Climate | Department of Energy
results.description: Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( ) or https:// means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. On July 29, 2025, the Department of Energy (DOE) published a report entitled A Critical Review of Impacts of Greenhouse Gas Emissions on the U.S. Climate, evaluating existing peer-reviewed literature and government data on climate impacts of Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions and providing a critical assessment of the conventional narrative on climate change.

results.title: ClimateChange | United Nations
results.description: COP30 concluded with an agreement on a wide range of climate issues among all 193 countries present at the conference, showing that nations can still come together to confront the defining challenges no country can solve alone.

results.title: How relevant is climate change research for climate change policy? An empirical analysis based on Overton data - PMC
results.description: In this study, we deal with the question of how research on climate change and policy are connected. According to Yin, Gao [11], the systematic understanding of the connection between science and policy is still limited, since reliable data are missing on a global scale. In 2019, however, the new Overton database of policy documents was released including links to research papers that are cited by policy documents.

results.title: Climate-change policy - Latest research and news | Nature
results.description: Climate policy portfolios reduce emissions but not fast enough for Paris Agreement goals. This study analyses 43 countries (2000–2022) and finds that specialised instruments, long-term targets, and dedicated governmental bodies are linked ...

results.title: N.I.H. Cuts Likely to Curtail Study of Climate Change’s Health Effects - The New York Times
results.description: But since Robert F. Kennedy Jr. took charge of H.H.S., the Trump administration has indicated that it will stop funding research on the health effects of climate change. The N.I.H. said in an internal document obtained by The New York Times that it was the agency’s new policy “not to prioritize” research related to climate change.

results.title: ON FAO STRATEGY CLIMATE CHANGE 2022–2031
results.description: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations · FAO. 2022. FAO Strategy on Climate Change 2022–2031. Rome

results.title: IPCC — Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change
results.description: IPCC reports are neutral, policy-relevant but not policy-prescriptive. The assessment reports are a key input into the international negotiations to tackle climate change. Created by the United Nations Environment Programme (UN Environment) and the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) in ...

results.title: Climate Change Is Erased From Reference Manual on Scientific Evidence for Judges - The New York Times
results.description: The fourth edition of the manual, totaling nearly 1,700 pages, was issued last year. The climate chapter remains available in the version posted to the National Academies’ website. A spokesperson said the organization, which publishes the manual under its National Academies Press, stood by the chapter.

No follow-up visits to outbound links were completed, so the agent did not retrieve or summarize any primary-source pages.
Required outputs (URL + summary + relation to the article) for the three target types — scientific paper, government policy, and organization position — are missing.
The merged results only list search-result landing pages; listing potential URLs without visiting them does not satisfy the task's evidence requirements.
Because there are no visited pages, the exploration path (article → links → target documents) is not documented.

details.exploration_quality: Fail — the agent performed an initial search but did not perform the necessary visits or extractions. To pass, the agent must (1) select and visit a specific news article about climate change, (2) follow its outbound links to and visit a scientific paper, a government policy document, and an organization position statement, (3) provide each target's URL and a concise summary, (4) explain how each relates to the original article, and (5) document the exploration path (sequence of visited URLs).
